what_it_does: Records metadata for an image attachment in the design-assets channel, mapping an attachment identifier to a target image URI and noting when it was added.
observations: The file consists only of YAML frontmatter with no additional body content
standouts: The target URI contains a space and parentheses in the path segment
The file is a bare ledger entry with only attachment metadata fields
The recorded addition timestamp is in 2026
concerns: The target URI contains a space and parentheses, which may cause parsing or escaping issues
No MIME type, file size, checksum, or display name is recorded
The 'added' timestamp is in the future relative to typical current dates, which may indicate a clock or data-entry issue
notes: This is a minimal attachment ledger entry that links an image attachment ID to a design-assets channel image URI and records its addition time.
questions: What is the file format, size, or checksum of the attached image?
Why is the added timestamp set to 2026?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file acts as an attachment ledger entry for an image in the design-assets channel, exactly as its id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields indicate.
intention_signals: To catalog an image asset attached to the design-assets channel so it can be referenced by attachment ID and linked to its stored image location.
novelty: The target URI includes a space and parentheses in the final path segment, which is an uncommon URI form.
contribution: Provides a persistent attachment identifier, channel context, attachment type, target URI, and creation timestamp for an image asset; a fused system would keep this linkage to locate and timestamp the attachment.
summary_for_embedding: Attachment ledger entry att-7989949f in the design-assets channel records an images-type attachment pointing to image://design-assets/icons/image (17) and added on 2026-06-22T12:26:24.971460+00:00.
intention_for_embedding: This file intends to register an image attachment in the design-assets channel so it can be referenced by ID and linked to its stored image URI.
